original: The "Epic in Miami," in 85°F heat, the San Diego Chargers defeat the Miami Dolphins 41-38 in overtime in the AFC Divisional Playoff Game, a game that sets numerous playoff scoring records
Most distant galaxy yet discovered found by scientists using Keck telescope in Hawaii (est 15 billion light years away)
Buffalo Bills player Damar Hamlin collapses in cardiac arrest and is revived by CPR on the field in televised NFL game against the Bengals in Cincinnati
USSR launches the Luna 1 spacecraft (Mechta), the 1st to leave Earth's gravity, reach the vicinity of the Moon and to be placed in heliocentric orbit
Record 19.2-foot alligator is shot in Louisiana by American businessman Edward Avery McIlhenny
Stardust successfully flies past Comet Wild 2, collecting samples that it will return to Earth two years later
Lynne Cox, American long-distance open-water swimmer known for easing Cold War tensions by swimming from the US to the Soviet Union in 1987, born in Boston, Massachusetts
Mariner 9 begins mapping Mars
Nevada Las Vegas basketball gives coach Jerry Tarkanian his 600th career victory with a 142-140 win over Utah State in triple overtime; total of 282 points breaks NCAA men's record of 275 set in 1976

NHL New York Islanders right wingMike Bossyscores his 499th and 500th career goals in the final 2:22 to lift the New York to a 7-5 victory over the Boston Bruins; 11th player in NHL history to score 500 goals
Welterweight Con Orem & heavyweight Hugh O'Neill brawl for 185 rounds before darkness ends legendary bare knuckle boxing match in Virginia City, Montana
Elara, a satellite of Jupiter, discovered by Charles Dillon Perrine in San Jose, California
First electron tube to enable night vision is described in St Louis, Missouri
University of Kentucky Wildcats men's basketball team begins 129 home game winning streak that only ends in 1955; incorporates NCAA titles in 1948, 1949 and 1951
The Beatles begin rehearsals, while being filmed, for the Let It Be project at Twickenham Film Studios, London
Martin Luther King Jr.begins a drive to register black voters
Lucia Zarate, Mexican entertainer (lightest known adult human - 2.1 kg at 17), born in Veracruz, Mexico (d. 1890)
Nina Kuscsik, American long-distance runner and officially the first woman to win the Boston Marathon, born in Brooklyn, New York (d. 2025)[1]
Robert B. Nemiroff's stage drama "To Be Young, Gifted & Black" adaptated from the writings of his late wife of Lorraine Hansberry, starring Ruby Dee, premieres in NYC
Mahatma Gandhibegins a march for peace in East Bengal
First Jewish child born in Spain since 1492 expulsion
New York Islanders beat Philadelphia Flyers, 3-1 at Nassau Veterans Memorial Coliseum to start 23 NHL game undefeated home streak (21-0-2), 14 straight wins
Willis Carrierreceives a US patent for the world's first air conditioner
Australian cricket spin bowling greatShane Warne(145 Tests, 708 wickets; 194 ODIs) makes Test debut v India in 3rd Test at Sydney Cricket Ground
Aleksandr Tikhonov, Russian biathlete (Olympic gold biathlon relay 1968, 72, 76, 80), born in Uyskoye, Chelyabinsk, Russia
Jack Hanna, American zookeeper and TV personality (Good Morning America; David Letterman), born in Knoxville, Tennessee
Lynda Barry, American cartoonist and author (Ernie Pook's Comeek, The Good Times are Killing Me), born in Richland Center, Wisconsin[1][2]
Naoki Urasawa, Japanese manga artist and author, various works sold over 140 million copies worldwide, born in Fuchū, Japan
Pernell Whitaker, American boxer (Olympic gold 1984, 4-division world champion), born in Norfolk, Virginia (d. 2019)
Anky van Grunsven, Dutch equestrian rider (Olympic gold dressage 2000, 04, 08; World C'ship gold 1994, 2006), born in Erp, Netherlands
Eric Whitacre, American Grammy Award-winning choral conductor and composer (Lux Aurumque; Deep Field), born in Reno, Nevada
Renée Elise Goldsberry, American actress and singer (Hamilton), born in San Jose, California
The Alamanni cross the frozen Rhine River in large numbers and invade the Roman Empire
World's Columbian Exposition opens in Chicago [or 0501]
First official Dutch Eleven Cities Skate (Minne Hoekstra in 13:50)
Australia releases its first national stamp - a kangaroo on a map of Australia
John Reynolds sets age of solar system at 4,950,000,000 years
Hawaii's then all-time low temperature, 14°F, is recorded atop Haleakalā
US Department of Interior designates grizzly bear a threatened species
Storm Eleanor forms near Ireland then sweeps across the UK and Western Europe in next few days

United States international Christian Pulisic becomes the most expensive American soccer player when he moves from Borussia Dortmund to Chelsea for £57.6M ($73M); remains at Dortmund on loan until the end of the season
First photo of the Moon, taken by French photographerLouis Daguerre
Christiaan Barnardperforms the world's second heart transplant on Philip Blaiberg
Michael van Gerwenof the Netherlands wins his 2nd PDC World Darts Championship beating defending champion Gary Anderson, 7-3 at the Alexandra Palace, London
Muhammad XII, the last Emir of Granada, surrenders his city toFerdinand II of Aragonand Isabella I of Castile, ending both the Reconquista and centuries of Muslim rule on the Iberian Peninsula
Lillian Leitzel [Leopoldina Alitza Pelikan], German acrobat and strongwoman, born in Breslau, Germany (d. 1931)
Kane Tanaka, oldest Japanese supercentenarian and 2nd oldest person in the world (119 years, 107 days), born in Wajiro Village, Japan (d. 2022)
Juanita Jackson Mitchell, American lawyer (1st African American to practice law in Maryland, NAACP), born in Hot Springs, Arkansas (d. 1992)
Barış Manço, Turkish rock guitarist, singer-songwriter (Kurtalan Ekspres - "Arkadaşım Eşek (My Friend the Donkey"), and television producer (From 7 to 77), born in Üsküdar, Istanbul, Turkey (d. 1999)
Wu Man, Chinese-American pipa (lute-like instrument) player, and composer (Silk Road Ensemble), born in Hangzhou, Zhejiang, China
William Fox-Pitt, English equestrian (Olympic silver team 2004, 12; bronze 2008; World Equestrian Games gold team 2010), born in London, England
Dmitri Shostakovich' 2nd Piano Concerto has US premiere, with Leonard Bernstein as soloist and conductor of the New York Philharmonic at Carnegie hall in NYCs in NY
First US wire suspension bridge for general traffic opens in Pennsylvania
George Washington's army hoists the Grand Union Flag at Prospect Hill, Charlestown
The SS Commodore, the shipStephen Craneis aboard, founders off the Florida coast, an experience he recounts in his short story "The Open Boat"
US and Canada agree to preserve the Niagara Falls
16-year-old future South African Test cricket batsman Darryl Cullinan scores his 1st first-class century (106 no) for Border against Natal B in East London

A brutal snowstorm smashes into the Midwestern United States, causing 14 inches (359 mm) of snow in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, and 19 inches (487 mm) in Chicago, where temperatures plunge to -13°F (-25°C); 68 deaths are reported
Larry Harmon, American entertainer and TV producer (Bozo the Clown), born in Toledo, Ohio (d. 2008)
Allen Sapp, Canadian Cree artist, born at Red Pheasant Reserve, Saskatchewan (d. 2015)[1]
Dawn Silva, American funk and R&B singer (Sly & The Family Stone; Parliament-Funkadelic; The Brides of Funkenstein - "Disco To Go"), born in Sacramento, California
Edgar Martínez, American Baseball HOF third baseman and DH (7 × MLB All-Star; 5 × Silver Slugger Award; AL batting champion 1992, 95; Seattle Mariners), born in New York City
Tia Carrere [Althea Janairo], American actress (Wayne's World, True Lies), born in Honolulu, Hawaii
Louis Charles Breguet, French aviation pioneer (founded the Compagnie des messageries aérienne which became Air France), born in Paris (d. 1955)
1st Curling club in US (Orchard Lake Curling Club) opens
First US commemoratives and first US stamp to picture a woman issued (Queen Isabella, patron of Columbus)
1st junior high schools in US opens (Berkeley California)
The M. H. de Young Memorial Museum of fine arts opens in Golden Gate Park, San Francisco, California
World War II: US government announces its Liberty ship program to build freighters in support of the war effort
First redshank, an Old World shorebird, reported in North America (Halifax)
Worst fire in Argentine history destroys 1.2 million acres
Sila Calderón becomes the first female Governor of Puerto Rico
Failed assassination attempt on PresidentKwame Nkrumahof Ghana
New York Jets sign future Pro Football Hall of Fame quarterbackJoe Namathto a $427,000 contract over three years (pro football record at the time)
Queen Emma of Hawaii, Consort of King Kamehameha IV (1856-63), born in Honolulu, Kingdom of Hawaii (d. 1885)
Helen Herron Taft, American First lady (1909-13) as wife of President William Howard Taft, planted Washington's cherry trees, born in Cincinnati, Ohio (d. 1943)
Apsley Cherry-Garrard, British Antarctic explorer and survivor of the Terra Nova Expedition (The Worst Journey in the World), born in Bedford, England (d. 1959)
Robert "Bob" Marshall, American forester, wilderness activist and co-founder of the Wilderness Society, born in New York City (d. 1939)
David Bailey, British fashion and portraiture photographer(Vogue; Alice Cooper "Billion Dollar Babies" album cover art), and director, born in Leytonstone, Essex, England
Glen Goins, American funk guitarist and singer (Parliament-Funkadelic - "Fantasy Is Reality"), born in Plainfield, New Jersey (d. 1978)
